---
title: "IoT Network Traffic Analysis with Machine Learning - Current Progress"
description: "An overview of my progress in my IoT-ML project: building a data pipeline from raw Zeek network logs to an engineered feature matrix for malicious traffic detection."
date: "2026-03-31"
categories: [machine learning, data-processing, data-pipeline, feature-engineering]
---

## Goal

Detect malicious network traffic from IoT devices using machine learning, based on Zeek network intrusion detection logs from 38 devices captured in November 2020

---

## Experimental Workflow

### 1. Data Ingestion

Raw Zeek JSON logs are extracted from 38 zip files and loaded into a SQLite database (~43M records across 14 tables), with traffic bucketed into time windows.

### 2. Feature Exploration

To determine which signals are worth encoding into the graph, log records are first aggregated into **34 candidate daily features** per device (connection counts, protocol ratios, DNS anomalies, file transfer stats). Redundant features are dropped by removing highly correlated pairs (|r| > 0.75) and high-multicollinearity features (VIF > 10), leaving **25 features** with distinct information. Derived security metrics — `traffic_asymmetry`, `src_concentration`, `weird_rate`, `dns_to_conn_ratio` — are identified here and carried forward as core signals in the graph. This step establishes *what to measure*; Step 3 builds those measurements directly into the graph at higher resolution.

### 3. Graph Construction

Going back to the raw logs, graphs are built at **hourly** granularity — each snapshot has devices as nodes and observed connections as edges, with two distinct types of features:

- **Edges** capture which devices connect to which, along with communication attributes — volume (`conn_count`, `orig_bytes`), reliability (`failed_ratio`), protocol mix (`tcp_ratio`), and application-layer behavior (`http_post_ratio`, `executable_files`, `tunnel_count`).
- **Node features describe how a device behaves overall** — aggregated across all its connections from both sender and receiver perspectives, enriched with DNS activity (query volume, NXDOMAIN ratio), and extended with temporal delta and rolling z-score features to flag sudden deviations from each device's recent 24-hour baseline.
- Input into a VGAE model

---

## Final Feature Set

| Category | Features |
|---|---|
| Connection | `conn_count`, `unique_src_ips`, `unique_dst_ips`, `unique_dst_ports`, `failed_conn_ratio` |
| Protocol | `tcp_ratio`, `udp_count`, `dns_count`, `dns_to_conn_ratio` |
| DNS / HTTP | `unique_domains`, `http_count`, `unique_hosts` |
| Files / SNMP / Network | `total_file_bytes`, `unique_mime_types`, `snmp_count`, `snmp_get_requests`, `snmp_set_requests`, `tunnel_count`, `dpd_count` |
| Derived Security | `traffic_asymmetry`, `total_bytes`, `src_concentration`, `weird_rate` |

---

## Current Status

| Phase | Status |
|---|---|
| Data ingestion pipeline | Done |
| Feature engineering | Done |
| EDA | Done |
| Graph prep | In-Progress |
| Model architecture / training | Not started |
| Evaluation / results | Not started |
